symptoms.symptom_name: Profound post-exertional crash with deep myalgia and cognitive failure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A delayed exertion-triggered crash unrelieved by rest on a post-Q-fever background is characteristic.

symptoms.symptom_name: Spouse-assisted FAST negative; global symmetric slowing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative FAST with symmetric global slowing argues strongly against acute stroke, though it is not a substitute for assessment if focal features emerge.
Afebrile with no cardiac symptoms lowers the probability of endocarditis relapse, though an echo is part of long-term care.
Rousability with normal breathing argues against a decompensating organic process.

symptoms.symptom_name: High dehydration risk (bedbound, needs help to drink)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe lethargy with difficulty drinking risks dehydration.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intact cranial nerves/reflexes, diffuse muscle tenderness, normal cardio/resp exam (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No focal deficit with a normal cardiorespiratory exam fits a post-infectious crash, not stroke/endocarditis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Documented prior Q fever with post-infectious fatigue

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Documented Q fever is the substrate for post-infectious fatigue crashes.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A severe exertion-triggered crash with a negative FAST, afebrile with no cardiac symptoms, rousable, on a post-Q-fever background, is a QFS crash — not stroke or endocarditis relapse. Management is radical rest and in-home hydration with a stroke/endocarditis screen and screening-bloods/echo pathway, avoiding a harmful ED transfer while addressing dehydration.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Spouse-assisted FAST and fever/cardiac-symptom screen (repeat if any change)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Key remote steps to safety-net stroke and endocarditis relapse.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-home/low-exertion bloods (FBC, CRP, U&E, ± Q fever serology) and outpatient echocardiogram
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude chronic Q fever endocarditis and metabolic contributors without provoking a crash.
Not indicated for a stereotyped crash with a clean screen; the trip may harm.
